title_en: Social and Economic Law in All-School Perspective
content_en:
The social and economic law sections of Al-Istidhkar cover the commercial, family, criminal, and social traditions of the Muwatta with the same comprehensive all-school comparative approach that characterizes the worship sections. The commercial law sections are particularly rich in cross-school comparison, since Islamic commercial law showed significant variation across the early schools — reflecting differences in the commercial customs of different regions and in the legal principles each school used to evaluate those customs.

Ibn Abd al-Barr's analysis of the commercial traditions in the Muwatta identifies the key principles underlying the Maliki approach to commercial law — the use of local custom as a subsidiary legal source, the doctrines of maslaha and istislah, and the Medinan practice (amal ahl al-Madinah) as an additional source of legal authority — and explains how these principles lead to positions that sometimes differ from those of other schools. His presentations of the Hanafi and Shafi'i alternatives are consistently accurate, making Al-Istidhkar a reliable guide to cross-school commercial jurisprudence.

The family law sections in Al-Istidhkar address the full range of domestic legal questions with particular attention to the points where Maliki law differs from other schools. The Maliki doctrine allowing the judge to dissolve a marriage in certain circumstances, the Maliki approach to the conditions of valid marriage contracts, and the specifically Maliki positions on divorce and its consequences are all articulated with their textual bases alongside the positions of other schools.

The criminal law sections document the cross-school positions on the fixed penalties and their conditions of application, the blood money calculations for various categories of injury, and the procedural requirements of Islamic criminal justice. Ibn Abd al-Barr's comparative analysis of these sensitive topics reflects both his deep knowledge of the legal tradition and his practical experience as a judge who had to apply these principles in real cases.
title_id: Hukum Sosial dan Ekonomi dari Perspektif Semua Mazhab
content_id:
Bagian-bagian hukum sosial dan ekonomi dari Al-Istidhkar mencakup tradisi-tradisi komersial, keluarga, pidana, dan sosial Muwatta' dengan pendekatan komparatif semua-mazhab yang komprehensif yang sama yang mencirikan bagian-bagian ibadah. Bagian-bagian hukum komersial sangat kaya dalam perbandingan lintas-mazhab, karena hukum komersial Islam menunjukkan variasi yang signifikan di antara mazhab-mazhab awal — mencerminkan perbedaan dalam adat komersial dari berbagai daerah dan dalam prinsip-prinsip hukum yang digunakan setiap mazhab untuk mengevaluasi adat-adat tersebut.

Analisis Ibn Abd al-Barr atas tradisi-tradisi komersial dalam Muwatta' mengidentifikasi prinsip-prinsip kunci yang mendasari pendekatan Maliki terhadap hukum komersial — penggunaan adat lokal sebagai sumber hukum tambahan, doktrin-doktrin maslahat dan istislah, dan praktik Madinah (amal ahl al-Madinah) sebagai sumber otoritas hukum tambahan — dan menjelaskan bagaimana prinsip-prinsip ini mengarah pada posisi-posisi yang terkadang berbeda dari mazhab-mazhab lain. Penyajiannya atas alternatif-alternatif Hanafi dan Syafi'i secara konsisten akurat, menjadikan Al-Istidhkar panduan yang dapat diandalkan untuk fikih komersial lintas-mazhab.

Bagian-bagian hukum keluarga dalam Al-Istidhkar membahas rentang penuh pertanyaan-pertanyaan hukum domestik dengan perhatian khusus pada titik-titik di mana hukum Maliki berbeda dari mazhab-mazhab lain. Doktrin Maliki yang memungkinkan hakim untuk membubarkan pernikahan dalam keadaan tertentu, pendekatan Maliki terhadap syarat-syarat kontrak pernikahan yang sah, dan posisi-posisi Maliki secara khusus tentang talak dan konsekuensinya semuanya diartikulasikan dengan dasar-dasar tekstualnya bersama posisi-posisi mazhab lain.

Bagian-bagian hukum pidana mendokumentasikan posisi-posisi lintas-mazhab tentang hukuman-hukuman tetap dan kondisi-kondisi penerapannya, perhitungan-perhitungan diyat untuk berbagai kategori cedera, dan persyaratan-persyaratan prosedural keadilan pidana Islam. Analisis komparatif Ibn Abd al-Barr atas topik-topik sensitif ini mencerminkan baik pengetahuannya yang mendalam tentang tradisi hukum maupun pengalaman praktisnya sebagai hakim yang harus menerapkan prinsip-prinsip ini dalam kasus-kasus nyata.
content_ar: The social and economic law sections of Al-Istidhkar cover the commercial, الأسرة, criminal, and social traditions of the الموطأ with the same comprehensive all-school comparative approach that characterizes the worship sections. The commercial law sections are particularly rich in cross-school comparison, بما أن Islamic commercial law showed المهم variation across the early schools — reflecting differences in the commercial customs of different regions and in the legal principles each school used to evaluate those customs. Ibn Abd al-Barr's analysis of the commercial traditions in the الموطأ identifies the الرئيسي principles underlying the المالكي approach to commercial law — the use of local custom as a subsidiary legal source, the doctrines of maslaha and istislah, and the Medinan practice (amal ahl al-المدينة المنورة) as an additional source of legal authority — and explains how these principles lead to positions that sometimes differ from those of other schools. His presentations of the الحنفي and الشافعي alternatives are consistently accurate, making Al-Istidhkar a reliable guide to cross-school commercial jurisprudence. The الأسرة law sections in Al-Istidhkar address the full range of domestic legal questions with particular attention to the points where المالكي law differs from other schools. The المالكي doctrine allowing the judge to dissolve a marriage in certain circumstances, the المالكي approach to the conditions of valid marriage contracts, and the specifically المالكي positions on divorce and its consequences are all articulated with their textual bases alongside the positions of other schools. The criminal law sections document the cross-school positions on the fixed penalties and their conditions of application, the blood المال calculations for various categories of injury, and the procedural requirements of Islamic criminal العدل. Ibn Abd al-Barr's comparative analysis of these sensitive topics reflects both his deep العلم of the legal التراث and his practical experience as a judge who had to apply these principles in real cases.
